utility.description: The feature provides meaningful and relevant benefit to the intended user.

rubric.pass: The feature delivers clear, measurable value to the intended user group.
rubric.fail: The feature provides no discernible benefit or is actively detrimental to users.
rubric.borderline: The benefit is ambiguous, minor, or only applies to a subset of users.

scoring_notes: Assess intended use, not hypothetical extreme misuse only.
Be conservative when evidence is missing.
Prefer borderline over pass when justification is unclear.

fairness.description: The feature avoids unjustified bias, exclusion, or unequal treatment across affected user groups.

rubric.pass: No group is systematically disadvantaged or excluded; outcomes are equitable.
rubric.fail: The feature introduces or perpetuates bias, discrimination, or unfair treatment.
rubric.borderline: Some groups may be indirectly disadvantaged, or fairness is not clearly demonstrated.

scoring_notes: Consider both direct and indirect impacts on different groups.
If data on group outcomes is missing, prefer borderline.
Explicitly check for proxy variables that may encode bias.

privacy.description: The feature respects data minimization, confidentiality, informed handling of personal data, and purpose limitation.

rubric.pass: Only necessary data is collected, stored securely, and used solely for its stated purpose.
rubric.fail: Unnecessary or sensitive data is collected, retained, or shared without justification.
rubric.borderline: Data practices are unclear, or retention/usage policies are not fully transparent.

scoring_notes: Check for explicit data minimization and retention policies.
If data flows are undocumented, prefer borderline or fail.
Consider both technical and organizational controls.

explainability.description: The feature provides users with enough clarity about how outputs, decisions, or relevant system behavior are produced for informed use.

rubric.pass: Users can easily understand why outputs or decisions are made and what data is used.
rubric.fail: The logic, criteria, or data usage is opaque or misleading to users.
rubric.borderline: Some explanations are provided, but key logic or data practices remain unclear.

scoring_notes: Assess explanations from the perspective of a typical end user.
If technical details are present but not accessible, prefer borderline.
Check for both completeness and clarity.

safety.description: The feature avoids causing or enabling reasonably foreseeable physical, psychological, social, legal, or financial harm.

rubric.pass: The feature does not expose users to harm, unsafe situations, or policy violations.
rubric.fail: The feature can cause physical, psychological, or financial harm, or violates safety policies.
rubric.borderline: Potential risks are present but not clearly addressed or mitigated.

scoring_notes: Consider both direct and indirect harms, including edge cases.
If risk mitigations are not documented, prefer borderline.
Do not assume users will always act safely; check for misuse potential.
